summary.intent: Work through Tier-3 open items (CI, --run-dir autosave, score history, spec-file loader), then dogfood the optimize-anything tool on a non-SKILL.md artifact to prove generalization.
summary.outcome: Discovered all Tier-3 items were already implemented; ran dogfood optimization on CLAUDE.md and README.md exposing three LLM judge bottlenecks; improved README (-118 lines), fixed a weight comment bug, designed an `analyze` subcommand, and produced handoff docs. Two commits made, unpushed.

repo: All four Tier-3 items (CI workflow, --run-dir autosave, score history, spec-file loader) were already code-complete in commit 9dc9448 — session notes were stale.
The weight comment in evaluators/skill_clarity.sh header (0.30/0.30/0.25/0.15) did not match the actual WEIGHTS dict (0.25/0.25/0.20/0.30); code is canonical.
docs/ directory is gitignored — HANDOFF.md and all design docs are local-only reference files.
gepa's default proposer model may be unavailable; --model must be passed explicitly on every optimize run.

222 tests collected total; doc contract tests in test_doc_contract.py enforce that README mentions specific schema terms (artifact_class, hard_constraints, evaluator_cwd, best_artifact, total_metric_calls, etc.).

Claude Sonnet as LLM judge produces highly stable scores (0.88 across 9+ independent evaluations of the same artifact), but exhibits conservatism — penalizes structural reorganization even when it improves UX.

Quality dimensions via --intake-json raise the scoring baseline from 0.88 to 0.9175 by giving the judge a more discriminating rubric; this is the right setup before any optimize run on near-ceiling artifacts.

code.finding: Header comment listed wrong weights; fixed to match actual WEIGHTS dict: structure=0.25, actionability=0.25, specificity=0.20, conciseness=0.30.
Enforces presence of schema terms in README; restructuring README broke these tests until terms were re-inserted into the evaluator runtime section.

workflow: Dogfooding on already-polished artifacts exposes judge bottlenecks, not tool failures — the tool correctly preserves quality when no mutation beats the original.

Three distinct LLM judge bottlenecks discovered in order: (1) vague objectives compress scoring range, (2) weak proposers make sweeping rewrites instead of surgical edits, (3) judge conservatism penalizes structural improvements.
Auto-refine / plateau-breaking is specifically a --judge-model + vague-objective problem; it does not apply to heuristic evaluators or well-specified quality dimensions.
The correct product framing is an `analyze` subcommand (pre-optimization dimension discovery) rather than a magic runtime loop.
Subagent-driven development pattern: handle trivial edits (comment fix, .gitignore) directly in the main session; dispatch subagents only for operational or interactive tasks.

friction: Tier-3 open items were listed as open in session notes but were already fully implemented — caused wasted scoping effort at session start.
gpt-4o-mini proposer consistently makes full-document rewrites rather than surgical improvements, making it ineffective as a proposer for near-ceiling, well-structured documents.
LLM judge conservatism: Sonnet's proposed README mutation was objectively better (quickstart-first, concise examples) but scored lower than the original due to judge penalizing restructuring.
README restructuring broke doc contract tests because condensed examples removed required schema terms; required a second edit pass to restore them.
Leftover optimized-*.md files from optimization runs needed manual cleanup after the session.
HANDOFF.md was not updated to point to the latest handoff doc — user caught this at session end.
open_items: Implement `analyze` subcommand: new CLI entry point + analyze_for_dimensions() in llm_judge.py that suggests quality dimensions before optimization.
Plateau detection advisory: post-run output when 0 improvements found, recommending quality dimensions or stronger proposer model.
Architecture diagram (deferred from previous session, still not built).
CI verification: confirm GitHub Actions secrets are configured and get a green run; 2 commits are unpushed.
Evaluate judge calibration for structural improvements — current judge penalizes reorganization; may need rubric guidance to value arrangement over completeness.
